spec_registration: M4 candidate 1, pre-registered on issue #42 comment 4950931158: the M4 module's hazard machinery refit per seed on the train half; K=20 mean-over-draws (streams 5200+k); start-wave weights; fresh_run_artifact_schema [20,8,5] with undefined-draw invalidation and dispersion disclosure; internal mixed-k reproduction cells + >=3 sigma anchor margins.
one_shot: one-shot scored run registered on issue #42 comment 4950931158; independent verification by rerun; publishes regardless; no holdout tuning
pre_registered_forecast: P(gate >= 4/5 seeds) = 0.55-0.75. The internal cells are reproduction cells for the very hazard family the floor priced (faithful OC 0.9689); the module analogs held the anchor statistics at 4.8-12.8 sigma on 100/100 halves. Named residual risks: the two thin recovery.60-66 cells and stock-vs-flow composition at prevalence.50-59 under k=4. Modal failure shape: one recovery.60-66 cell on 2 seeds.

verdict.rule: seed passes iff every gated cell holds (internal |ln(rbar/rate_a)| <= tolerance AND anchor margin >= MARGIN_K=3 sigma); gate passes iff >= 4 of 5 gate seeds pass

internal.note: reproduction cells: |ln(rbar_candidate / rate_a)| vs round(floor mean + k*sd, 3), flow k=3 / stock k=4

anchor.note: concept-bridged invariants on the candidate's simulated holdout: prevalence min adjacent gap; retirement-exit dominance margin; each > MARGIN_K=3 x real half-split sd

prevalence_ageshape.comonotone|female.statistic: prevalence min adjacent gap over bridged bands

prevalence_ageshape.comonotone|male.statistic: prevalence min adjacent gap over bridged bands

fit_meta.estimator: unmodified populace_dynamics.data.disability weighted band x sex reference rates on the train half (side B); start-wave weights; no free hyperparameter

per_draw_per_cell_rates.rule: r[k, cell, s] for all K=20 draws (default_rng(5200+k)), all 8 gated internal cells, all 5 gate seeds -- so rbar and |ln(rbar/rate_a)| recompute cell-by-cell, not on trust

per_draw_dispersion_disclosure.note: REPORT-ONLY: no dispersion cap gates the run; it lets a referee see whether a passing 20-draw mean conceals a wild individual draw.

protocol.candidate_estimator: mean-over-K=20-draws (5200+k), scored ONCE per cell as |ln(rbar_candidate,s / rate_a,s)| -- not the mean of per-draw scores
protocol.split: person-disjoint 50/50 (split_panel_by_person, fraction=0.5, seed=s); side A holdout, side B train complement
protocol.weight_definition: START-WAVE cross-sectional PSID weight of each transition pair (the pinned M4 convention); no unweighted gated statistic
protocol.internal_statistic: |ln(rbar_candidate,s / rate_a,s)| <= round(floor mean + k*sd, 3) capped at ln(1.5); flow k=3, stock k=4
protocol.anchor_statistic: candidate's own per-seed simulated invariant on the rbar basis (prevalence min adjacent gap; retirement-exit dominance margin) > MARGIN_K=3 x the committed real half-split sd -- the margin reading
protocol.conjunction: seed passes iff every gated cell holds; gate passes iff >= 4 of 5 gate seeds

fresh_run_artifact_schema.undefined_draw_rule: any undefined gated draw (empty simulated denominator) INVALIDATES the run; enforced as a hard stop before write

model.description: the M4 module's hazard machinery: fit the unmodified populace_dynamics.data.disability weighted band x sex incidence / recovery / prevalence rates + the FRA-window retirement-exit share on the train half (side B); simulate the holdout support (side A) forward and score the same reference_moments / prevalence_shares / conversion_exit_shares code the floor used. No free hyperparameter.
